title: Traveling with Kyle - Thailand, Malasia, Indonesia

transcript.text: All right, so I went to Bangkok. This is the second time I was in Bangkok. The first time I was in Bangkok was on my way to Korea, and I was there just a couple days, but this time I had found out about, I knew about, what's it called, Khao San Road, which is where all the cheap hostels are and stuff, so I went down there and I stayed there, and it's like laying this road, there's all the tours, and they had like these movies you could watch outside, and for a couple days I was riding around, for some stupid reason I didn't do the Thai massage over at,

Khao Po, what is that called, Khao Pat? School, the big most famous Thai massage school. I didn't do that for some reason, but I did do the tour of the palace, which is beautiful, because it's like, like all these different temples all next to each other behind this big wall, and they're plated in gold, and I guess their, their dynasty, or their, they had their king there up until like World War I or something, and

It was very ornate and pretty, and I didn't do any of the sex tours and stuff, I didn't, for some reason I didn't even know about that, and I probably would have if I did, but I did have a day where I rode around the city, because I had to,

I had to go to an embassy to do something, get like a new passport, or I can't remember what it was, but I rode across town, and it was very polluted, it wasn't as bad as, it was not as unhealthy as Kathmandu, because Kathmandu has these little tuk-tuk things that spew like blackness all over your face,

I guess the ones that they use in Nepal are even illegal in India, they're like air, building air conditioning, um, air conditioner machines, the power of the engines, but, uh, Kathmandu was really bad, it was up there, um, not a very healthy place to ride around, but, um, I had fun anyways,

Because there's so much traffic on a bike, you can just weave through everybody and run, run lights, and you can get around pretty fast, and so then I left Kathmandu, and I went, uh, I mean, Bangkok, and I rode down towards the, towards, uh, the islands, so during the day I'd ride, and then I'd stay at a hotel, and I noticed that out that the, that the Thailand, Thailanders, what do they call it, the Thai, don't really clean up their beaches in their place, there's lots of, like, just trash and stuff,

But I tried to take a boat to this island called Wat, called Kalfak, or Kofangang, or something, from one town, but there was too many, too many waves, and I guess a boat sank or something, so I went to another place,

And I got sick of riding at one point, and I took my bike, and, uh, actually caught some guy, red-handed, the guy who works on the bus, stealing tapes out of my bag, I actually took it out of his hand,
And I didn't say anything to him, or the people he worked with, though, I should have, but, uh, then I got out, and I took a boat down to Kofangang,
And I wanted to do the, and I, oh, and I stayed there on some, on the northern side of the island, there was a small, like, you know, series of little huts that I could stay in,
There was all these tourists there, and the lady who was running the huts sold mushrooms to everybody, magic mushrooms,
So everybody was tripping, and we hung out on the beach all night long, and I think it was a full moon,
And there was, uh, people were having a blast, it was really fun, there was one couple there who was just cracking jokes the whole time,
Like, people were walking by, and they were saying, oh, look, another moving postcard, but it was fun,
And then I went down to the southern part of it, where there was this gym and stuff, and I was going to do the scuba diving,
But I said, oh, yeah, I kind of have asthma, and they were, like, Germans, and they freaked out, and they didn't want to let me,
So I went down to Costa Muna instead, and I did this scuba diving down there, and, uh, but it wasn't very interesting,
Because it was right after a storm, and I could only see, like, a couple feet in front of me,
Uh, we did take a boat out farther and saw a little bit, but, um, and then I rode, I took the boat back to the mainland
And rode my bike down, and actually met up with some guy who was bike touring north, and we went into some town
And shared this hotel room one night, uh, to save money, and even though we were going in opposite directions,
And he'd done that same tour, like, 10 years beforehand, he just wanted to do the same one,
The same route again, or something, and, uh, and then I went down, um, to Georgetown,
And, uh, which I guess is, yeah, and, um, yeah, and then I crossed into Malaysia, that's right,
And rode down Malaysia, and Malaysia, I noticed was, I guess this is another video, but I'll include it,
Malaysia was definitely distinct from Thailand, it was more upscale, it was kind of like a first world
Country, the roads were in a lot better shape, but there was a lot of, uh, uh, creep, uh, um, clear-cutting,
I could see whole patches of just clear-cut, and then I'd go through another patch of, uh,
A forest where all the trees were planted in a row, and, um, but it was cool to be riding on nicer roads,
And, uh, stayed in some kind of hotels on the way down, and went to Georgetown, which is Malaysia,
And, uh, I got sick, I was having lung problems, I think probably from just being around all these,
Like, bat-polluted cars, and I went into a hospital in Georgetown, and they have a good, uh,
Health care system, they gave me some inhalers, and some antibiotics, and stuff,
And I took the boat over to, uh, Sumatra in Indonesia, and, uh, rode my bike from wherever I got off,

But I guess there was some orangutan sanctuary there, and I did stay at the town there, though,
It was one of the biggest, I think it was like the third biggest town and city in all of Indonesia,
